content:
I run a web development agency and we’ve mostly been building WordPress sites with Astra + Elementor. Our clients like Elementor because after handoff they can make basic edits themselves without needing us for every text or image change.
Lately we’ve been experimenting a lot with Cursor/Claude and the results are honestly impressive. We’ve been able to build some really nice static sites much faster, and we’ve also been building more custom app-style projects using React, MariaDB, and Tailwind.
So now we’re at a bit of a crossroads.
AI-assisted static builds are way faster and cleaner, but the client loses the easy editing experience unless they know HTML/CSS or keep us on retainer for every small update. On the other hand, the WordPress/Elementor workflow is slower and less clean, but clients understand it and like being able to edit the site after launch.
Has anyone found a good middle ground?
A few things I’m wondering:
Can Cursor/Claude realistically generate Elementor-compatible JSON templates that can be imported into Elementor?
Is it better to use AI for the design/static prototype, export to Figma and then rebuild manually in Elementor?
Are agencies starting to phase out Elementor for static sites and just selling managed updates?
Is there a good static-site CMS workflow that does not turn into building a whole custom WordPress replacement?
How are people handling SEO in AI-built/static sites? We do a lot of Yoast-based SEO now, so we’d still need proper heading structure, SEO titles, meta descriptions, schema, image alt text, etc.
I’m not trying to bash Elementor. It solves a real client problem. But AI is making the design/build side so much faster that it feels like the old workflow may not be the best fit for every project anymore.
We just did a 15k 80 page website for a client that took 3 months. One round of revisions took a week and it was a small edit x 80 pages. It sure would have been nice to prompt Cursor to go and do the edit for us on all 80 pages. That would have taken 5 minutes, not a week.
Would love to hear how other agencies or freelancers are handling this.

  submitted by   /u/flight_of_the_condor
[link]   [comments]
